title: viola cornuta
intro: Viola cornuta, also known as the bird's mouth or bird's beak violet, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the violet family. The roots and leaves of the Viola cornuta plant have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as sore throats, fever, and digestive problems. The roots of the plant can be prepared into a tea or tincture, while the leaves can be used fresh in salads or dried for later use in herbal preparations.
